summary: Added a C implementation and public declaration for sysdolphin baselib function hsd_803A949C, a CARD/memory-card related callback or command-completion handler. The implementation introduced local reconstructed structs for command entries, extended card state, and card data blocks, then handled many command IDs by closing CARD files, validating or copying sector/header/icon/data contents, updating per-slot metadata arrays, computing checksums via existing hsd_803B2B20, and advancing a 128-entry command queue. decomp.dev reported hsd_803A949C improved from 0.00% to 58.78% matched, so this was a substantial but not complete decompilation step.

key_files.role: Added the hsd_803A949C implementation, static helpers, local reconstructed structs, CARD close retry logic, command switch handling, data validation/copy paths, checksum calls, and queue advancement.
Added the external prototype for void hsd_803A949C(s32 arg1).

what_changed: Created src/sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3A94.c content where the file was effectively empty before, adding 491 lines.
Included hsd_3A94.h, hsd_3AA7.h, hsd_3B2B.h, hsd_3B2E.h, dolphin/card.h, and string.h.
Declared external globals hsd_804D7980, hsd_804D7988, and hsd_804D799C used as queue index/status/error state.
Added local reconstructed structs CardStateExt, CardCmdEntry, and CardBlock with explicit offset comments and padding fields.
Added static helper hsd_803A949C_GetEntry to index ((CardCmdEntry*) hsd_804D1148)[hsd_804D7980].
Added static helper hsd_803A949C_Close that retries CARDClose up to 10 times while the result is -1.
Added static helper hsd_803A949C_IconSize mapping state->x3B0 value 2 to 0x1800, value 1 to 0xE00, and default to 0.
Added static helper hsd_803A949C_FileId to combine CardBlock bytes x10 and x11 into a 16-bit file ID.
Implemented hsd_803A949C(s32 arg1) as a large switch on entry->x0, with cases 1, 2, 5, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 13, 15, and 16 visible in the diff.
Handled error argument propagation by closing the CARD file in most active command cases and assigning hsd_804D7988 to arg1 or other negative/status codes.
Used hsd_803B31CC to validate card buffer regions before consuming copied block data in several command paths.
Used memcpy and memcmp for header/icon/data payload transfer and verification, including special handling for the first sector versus later sectors.
Used hsd_803B2B20 to compute or update 0x30-byte checksum/trailer data stored at state->x430 indexed by entry->x8 * 0x10.
Updated per-slot arrays state->x170 and state->x270 for command cases that record file IDs or metadata, including sentinel value -0x7FFF for invalid slots.
At function exit, reset entry->x0 to 0, set hsd_804D799C to 0, and increment hsd_804D7980 modulo 128.
smart_moves: Kept uncertain reverse-engineered data layouts local to hsd_3A94.c instead of prematurely exporting poorly understood structs in a public header.
Used explicit offset comments and padding fields in CardStateExt, CardCmdEntry, and CardBlock, which makes later struct refinement safer and searchable.
Factored repeated close, icon-size, queue-entry, and file-ID behavior into static helpers, reducing duplication inside the large switch and improving match-guided iteration.
Preserved observable CARD error behavior with a close retry loop and command-specific assignments to hsd_804D7988 rather than flattening all failures into one code.
Modeled first-sector versus subsequent-sector layout differences explicitly with hdr_plus_icon, sector_size, remaining, and data_offset variables.
Left many unknown fields named by offset-style xNN members, avoiding speculative semantic names before stronger evidence is available.
Advanced the command ring index with modulo 128 at the end, matching the reconstructed queue size implied by hsd_803A949C_GetEntry.
decomp_lessons: For large unmatched HSD/card routines, landing a partial but structured C implementation can still materially improve progress; decomp.dev reported hsd_803A949C rose from 0.00% to 58.78%.
When decompiling callback-style IO handlers, keep global status variables and cleanup side effects in the same ordering as the assembly, especially close calls before error propagation.
Offset-commented local structs are useful when a function depends on many fields but the full owning type is not yet known.
Repeated library-call patterns such as CARDClose retries can be isolated into static helpers without exposing new ABI surface.

Memory-card file formats in this area appear to mix a 0x40 header, variable icon data of 0xE00 or 0x1800 bytes, payload sectors, and a 0x30 checksum/trailer block; future work should verify these semantics before renaming fields.
Use existing helper functions hsd_803B31CC and hsd_803B2B20 rather than inlining guessed validation/checksum behavior.
Unknown command IDs can still be represented as numeric switch cases if that preserves control flow and matching progress; semantic names should wait for stronger cross-reference evidence.
naming_conventions: Primary function retained address-based name hsd_803A949C.
Static helpers used address-prefixed names plus descriptive suffixes: hsd_803A949C_GetEntry, hsd_803A949C_Close, hsd_803A949C_IconSize, and hsd_803A949C_FileId.
Unidentified struct fields used x-offset naming such as x0, x8, x3B0, x430, and x460.
Padding fields used pad_offset naming such as pad_04, pad_28, pad_3B1, pad_00, and pad_13.
Local reconstructed type names were descriptive but cautious: CardStateExt, CardCmdEntry, and CardBlock.
Extern globals retained address-based symbols such as hsd_804D7980, hsd_804D7988, hsd_804D799C, hsd_804D1148, hsd_804D7994, and hsd_804D79A8.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used a single large switch over entry->x0 to mirror the original command dispatcher.
Introduced small static helpers where repeated instruction sequences likely corresponded to shared operations: queue entry selection, close retry, icon-size branch, and two-byte file ID construction.
Used explicit signed and unsigned comparisons, including casts such as (u32) remaining > sector_size and (s32) block->x12, to preserve branch behavior.
Preserved literal error/status constants including -0x105, -0x107, 2, -1, and -0x7FFF.
Preserved sector arithmetic with expressions such as (state->x24 + 0x30) % state->x8, icon_size + 0x40, and sector_size * entry->x8 - 0x40 - icon_size.
Used void-pointer casts around entry fields passed to memcpy and memcmp, consistent with command entries still being represented as integer-like unknown fields.
Limited header exposure to the final function prototype; tentative structs and helper functions stayed private to the C file.

review_feedback.feedback: Automated report for GALE01 showed one unmatched-item improvement: main/sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3A94 item hsd_803A949C gained 2852 bytes and improved from 0.00% to 58.78%.
No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching src/sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3A94.c to see whether hsd_803A949C reached a full match.
Search for hsd_804D1148 and hsd_804D7980 to identify the full command queue type and whether CardCmdEntry was later renamed or exported.
Search for hsd_803B31CC and hsd_803B2B20 callers to confirm whether they are checksum, validation, or block trailer helpers.
Inspect surrounding hsd_3AA7, hsd_3B2B, and hsd_3B2E symbols for stronger names for CardStateExt fields x170, x270, x370, x430, and x460.
Look for later review or matching notes about command IDs 1, 2, 5, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 13, 15, and 16 in this card subsystem.
